title: Extended formal verification: complete gfp proofs and expand to gf2m
description:
Complete the remaining gaps in gfp/ formal verification and expand coverage to gf2m/ arithmetic.

## Scope

**gfp/ completion (Lean4):**
- Eliminate the sole remaining sorry (`p_inv_value_spec`) by proving the Newton iteration loop invariant
- Prove binary exponentiation (`mod_pow_mont`) correctness
- Prove `inv()` via Fermat's little theorem, completing the full Fp arithmetic chain
- After this, every Fp operation (new, value, add, sub, mul, neg, inv, div, pow) has a machine-checked proof

**bitvec/ expanded coverage (Kani):**
- Add Kani harnesses for remaining mutating operations (shift_right, bit_and_into, bit_or_into, set, clear)
- Lean4 not feasible for bitvec (Vec-backed array indexing)

**gf2m/ new coverage:**
- Assess Charon/Aeneas extraction feasibility for the gf2m module (UintExt sealed trait, Arc params)
- Prove schoolbook multiplication (`mul_raw`) and inverse correctness in Lean4 if extraction works
- Kani harnesses for table-based operations (log/exp table validity) where Lean4 is not feasible

## Principles

- Kani only when Lean4 is not feasible (array indexing, dynamic allocation, SIMD)
- No duplication: gfp Montgomery bounds, extension field Karatsuba, and gfpn arithmetic are already universally proven in Lean4 — no redundant Kani harnesses for those
- All proofs track the actual Rust code via Charon/Aeneas extraction or inline `#[cfg(kani)]` harnesses
